traceReason: feature/ branches require auditable traces for repository-affecting work
taskSummary: Define compliance scope and system boundary as part of a broader SOC 2 and ISO/IEC 27001 readiness workstream.
implementationSummary: Created a SOC 2 and ISO/IEC 27001 readiness documentation folder.
Defined the ResearchOps compliance scope and system boundary across service, data, users, suppliers, integrations and controls.
Added an evidence index that records current evidence and gaps before any compliance claim can be made.
Added a repository test to prevent unsupported compliance or certification wording.
Added a GOV.UK-rendered compliance readiness page at /pages/compliance-readiness/.
Added a GOV.UK footer link to the page without adding it to the main service navigation.
Added a formal internal readiness matrix mapped to SOC 2 Trust Services Criteria categories and ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Annex A controls.
Updated the GOV.UK render workflow to track the compliance-readiness data module.
Registered the compliance readiness page in the visual walkthrough registry after CI showed the route was discoverable but uncovered.
Addressed browser review comments by keeping the compliance readiness page authored in Nunjucks, rendered through GOV.UK Frontend macros and styled through the GOV.UK Sass build.
Switched the warning text to the official GOV.UK warning-text macro with HTML content so abbreviation markup renders inside the component.
Added a shared Nunjucks abbreviation filter for SOC, TSC, ISO/IEC, DPIA, ROPA, SLOs and RTO/RPO.
Replaced visible bullet lists in the SOC 2 TSC and ISO/IEC Annex A matrix columns with comma-separated reference spans.
Added small GOV.UK text utility classes to the control matrix and a minimal Sass hook for top-aligned matrix cells and non-wrapping control references.
Updated the generated GOV.UK page test helper so route-state tests render pages with the same shared abbreviation filter as the static renderer.
Addressed follow-up browser review by removing abbreviation markup from the main page heading while retaining abbreviation markup in explanatory content and matrix headings.
Addressed follow-up browser review by scoping warning-icon alignment to the compliance readiness warning component.
Changed control-reference rendering so commas belong to the preceding reference span and do not render with a space before the comma.
Added GDPR abbreviation markup through the shared compliance abbreviation filter.
Changed header and footer includes to bypass stale browser cache so the footer support link to /pages/compliance-readiness/ is visible after deployment updates.
Reworked the footer cache-busting follow-up from a page-specific compliance readiness key to a shared generated GOV.UK page chrome cache key applied to all rendered pages.

residualRisks: The proposed boundary requires Home Office owner and security sign-off.
The branch does not create a full asset inventory, risk register, supplier assurance pack, SOC 2 mapping or ISO/IEC 27001 Statement of Applicability.
No SOC 2 compliance or ISO/IEC 27001 certification claim should be made from this PR alone.

The page uses category-level SOC 2 TSC mappings and selected ISO/IEC 27001 Annex A control references for readiness; final assessment needs assessor confirmation at criteria/control and evidence level.
